sections.heading: 1. Introduction
content: This Cancellation Policy outlines the terms and conditions under which service agreements and projects can be cancelled by either Adymire Agency ("the Company") or the client.
This policy applies to all projects and services including UI/UX Design, Mobile App Development, Web Development, Digital Marketing, and AI Automation & AI-Based Solutions.
By entering into a service agreement with us, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to the terms outlined in this Cancellation Policy.

sections.heading: 2. Key principle: Cancellation rights
content: IMPORTANT NOTICE:
The right to cancel a service agreement or project contract rests solely and exclusively with Adymire Agency (the Company).

While clients may request cancellation, the final decision to accept or reject such requests is at the Company's discretion based on the circumstances of each case, work completed, resources invested, and business considerations.
This policy is designed to protect both parties while acknowledging the unique nature of software development and creative services.

sections.heading: 3. Company-initiated cancellation
content: The Company reserves the right to cancel or terminate a project or service agreement under the following circumstances:

subSections.heading: 3.1 Policy violations
bullets: If the client violates any terms of the service agreement
If the client engages in abusive, threatening, or inappropriate behavior toward Company staff
If the client provides false or misleading information
If the client fails to comply with agreed-upon policies and procedures

subSections.heading: 3.2 Payment issues
bullets: Non-payment or late payment of invoices beyond the agreed grace period
Chargebacks initiated without prior communication with the Company
Bounced checks or failed payment transactions
Refusal to pay for completed work or deliverables

subSections.heading: 3.3 Scope creep and unreasonable requests
bullets: If the client repeatedly requests changes beyond the agreed scope without accepting additional charges
If project requirements change so significantly that the original agreement no longer applies
If the client makes unrealistic demands or sets impossible deadlines

subSections.heading: 3.4 Client unavailability
bullets: If the client fails to provide required information, assets, or feedback within reasonable timeframes
If the client is unresponsive to communication attempts for an extended period (typically 30+ days)
If lack of client cooperation prevents project progress

subSections.heading: 3.5 Legal or ethical concerns
bullets: If the project involves illegal activities or content
If the project conflicts with the Company's values or ethical standards
If the project poses legal risks to the Company

subSections.heading: 3.6 Business reasons
bullets: If the Company is unable to deliver the service due to internal constraints
If the project is no longer commercially viable for the Company
If unforeseen circumstances make project completion impossible

subSections.heading: When the Company initiates cancellation:
bullets: Written notice will be provided to the client
Payment will be settled based on work completed to date
Any deliverables completed will be provided to the client (if payments are settled)
Refund decisions will be made in accordance with the Refund Policy

sections.heading: 4. Client-requested cancellation
content: Clients may REQUEST cancellation of a project, but such requests are subject to Company approval.
The Company will evaluate each request based on:
bullets: Project stage and work completed
Resources and time already invested
Third-party costs incurred
Availability of alternative solutions

subSections.heading: 4.1 Cancellation request process
content: STEP 1: Submit a written cancellation request via email with:
• Project details and invoice number
• Reason for cancellation
• Preferred resolution (refund, service credit, etc.)
STEP 2: Company will review the request within 7-14 business days.
STEP 3: Company will provide a decision and settlement terms.
STEP 4: If approved, final payments and deliverables will be processed.

subSections.heading: 4.2 Cancellation charges
content: If a client-requested cancellation is approved, the following charges may apply:
BEFORE PROJECT COMMENCEMENT:
• If work has not started : Deposit may be partially or fully refunded at Company's discretion
• Administrative fees and third-party costs are non-refundable
DURING PROJECT EXECUTION:
• Client will be charged for all work completed up to the cancellation date
• Calculation will be based on deliverables completed, time invested, and resources used
• Minimum charges may apply even if deliverables are not complete
AFTER PROJECT COMPLETION:
• No cancellation is possible once the project is delivered and accepted
• Requests for cancellation after delivery will not be entertained

subSections.heading: 5.1 Discovery/planning phase (0-20% Complete)
bullets: Cancellation may be approved with minimal charges
Deposit and discovery fees are typically non-refundable
Any research, planning documents, or wireframes created remain Company property unless purchased separately

subSections.heading: 5.2 Design/development phase (20-80% Complete)
bullets: Cancellation approval is subject to evaluation
Client will be charged for all completed work and work in progress
Partial deliverables may be provided upon full payment settlement
No refunds for work already completed

subSections.heading: 5.3 Testing/delivery phase (80-100% Complete)
bullets: Cancellation requests at this stage are rarely approved
If approved, full payment for the project is required
Deliverables will be provided as per agreement

sections.heading: 6. Mutual cancellation agreement
content: In some cases, both parties may mutually agree to cancel a project. Terms will be documented in writing, work completed will be:
bullets: Documented in writing with clear settlement terms
Work completed will be paid for
Intellectual property rights will be clearly defined
Both parties release each other from further obligations

sections.heading: 7. No-cancellation scenarios
content: Cancellation is NOT permitted in the following cases:
bullets: After final deliverables have been provided and accepted
If the client has received substantial value from the work
If custom development or design work has been completed
If third-party costs have been incurred on the client's behalf
If the cancellation request is made in bad faith

sections.heading: 8. Consequences of cancellation
content: When a project is cancelled:

subSections.heading: 8.1 Payment settlement
bullets: All outstanding invoices must be paid
Charges for completed work will be calculated and billed
Third-party costs will be passed to the client
Refunds (if any) will be processed as per the Refund Policy

subSections.heading: 8.2 Intellectual property
bullets: Work-in-progress materials remain the property of the Company unless purchased separately
Source code, design files, and proprietary materials will not be shared unless full payment is made

subSections.heading: 8.3 Confidentiality
bullets: Both parties remain bound by confidentiality obligations
Project-related information must not be disclosed publicly

subSections.heading: 8.4 Future Engagement
bullets: The Company reserves the right to decline future projects from clients with a history of cancellations
Blacklisting may occur in cases of severe policy violations

sections.heading: 9. Subscription and recurring services
content: For subscription-based or recurring services:
bullets: Cancellation requests must be submitted before the next billing cycle
No refunds for the current billing period
Services continue until the end of the paid period
Reactivation may require a new setup fee

sections.heading: 10. Force majeure
content: Cancellation or suspension of services may occur due to events beyond our control, including:
• Natural disasters
• Pandemics or health emergencies
• War, terrorism, or civil unrest
• Government actions or legal restrictions
• Technology failures or cyber attacks
In such cases:
• Both parties will be released from obligations temporarily or permanently
• Payments will be settled based on work completed
• Alternative arrangements may be negotiated

sections.heading: 11. Dispute resolution
content: If there is a dispute regarding cancellation:
STEP 1: Direct communication and negotiation between parties
STEP 2: Mediation through a neutral third party (if agreed)
STEP 3: Arbitration or legal proceedings as a last resort
sections.note: The Company prefers to resolve disputes amicably and will make reasonable efforts to find mutually acceptable solutions.

sections.heading: 12. Notice period
bullets: Client-requested cancellations: Minimum 14 days' written notice recommended
Company-initiated cancellations: Minimum 7 days' written notice (except for severe violations)

sections.heading: 13. Changes to this policy
content: We reserve the right to update or modify this Cancellation Policy at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting on our website.
Your continued use of our services after changes are made constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.heading: 14. Contact information
content: For cancellation requests or questions about this policy, please contact us:
Adymire Agency
Email: [Your Official Email]
Phone: [Your Official Phone Number]
Website: [Your Website URL]
Address: [Your Registered Business Address]

sections.heading: 15. Acceptance of this policy
content: By making a payment or engaging our services, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to this Refund Policy.
If you do not agree with any part of this policy, please do not proceed with payment or service engagement.
